file_insights.coverage: Comprehensive architecture documentation bridging current (host-side Nushell adapters) and target (QEMU VM-based) states

patterns_discovered.context: QEMU VMs with 9P filesystem mounts replace host-side process spawning
patterns_discovered.scope: Architectural shift from Wave 1-4 to Wave 5+

patterns_discovered.context: 5-argument Nushell signature: workdir, prompt_file, stdout_log, stderr_log, memory_out
patterns_discovered.invariant: All adapters follow same interface regardless of underlying tool (claude, ollama, qemu, etc.)

patterns_discovered.pattern: Exit code semantics for dispatcher routing
patterns_discovered.detail: Rate-limit escape hatch via exit 75 enables automatic retries

patterns_discovered.context: Card bundle mounted read-write into VM; zam reads prompt, writes output/result.md
patterns_discovered.alignment: MICROCLAW principle: card bundle IS the IPC

patterns_discovered.context: All 8 adapters + 16 system scripts migrated to .nu (2026-03-03)

gotchas_discovered.detail: Dispatcher may pass relative paths; adapters must resolve to absolute before changing directory
gotchas_discovered.code_pattern: let workdir_abs = if ($workdir | str starts-with "/") { $workdir } else { [$orig_dir $workdir] | path join }

gotchas_discovered.detail: Must check both stderr content ("429", "rate limit") AND exit code (142→75 for timeout)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incomplete detection leads to false permanent failures instead of retries

gotchas_discovered.detail: macOS uses perl alarm; Linux may differ. Exit code 142 (SIGALRM) must map to 75
Host-side adapters (claude.nu, ollama.nu, etc.) will be replaced by qemu.nu in Wave 5; not future-proof
gotchas_discovered.note: Documentation marks them as "valid" but temporary

gotchas_discovered.detail: dub (UEFI bootloader with TPM attestation) integration planned Wave 6+; qemu.nu currently boots zam directly
gotchas_discovered.impact: Current VM architecture lacks measured boot security layer

approach_outcome.result: Comprehensive documentation establishing VM-first architecture as target state, with clear transition path from current host-side adapters
quality_signals: Clear ASCII diagrams showing dispatcher → adapter → VM flow
Concrete examples of calling convention, path resolution, rate-limit detection
9P filesystem semantics explained with directory structure example
Future roadmap (dub → zam boot chain in Wave 6)
approach_outcome.alignment_with_memory: Adheres to bop project memory: MICROCLAW (card=IPC, UART=stdout), Nushell canonical, zam/dub references correct

recommendations.rationale: Current architecture diagrams show components; readers need complete flow: card pending → dispatcher → adapter spawn → qemu-system-x86_64 → zam reads 9P → result.md written → VM exit → card moved to done/
recommendations.impact: Reduces cognitive load for new contributors

recommendations.recommendation: Document UART output structure (JSON lines format)
recommendations.rationale: README mentions "zam outputs JSON to UART" but doesn't specify schema; critical for metrics/telemetry pipeline
recommendations.impact: Enables structured logging, dispatch status tracking, rate-limit detection

recommendations.recommendation: Add troubleshooting section for adapter failures
recommendations.rationale: Common issues: relative path not resolved, stderr parsing fails, qemu not in PATH, 9P mount permission denied
recommendations.impact: Reduces debugging friction for operator and developer

recommendations.recommendation: Link to zam/dub project wikis or CONTRIBUTINGs
recommendations.rationale: "See /Users/studio/zam/" is opaque; explicit links to auto-claude specs, Wave roadmaps help contributors prioritize
9P latency, throughput, and cold-start VM time affect card SLA; MICROCLAW trades process spawning overhead for isolation overhead
recommendations.impact: Informs Wave 5 rollout decision (when is qemu.nu faster than host-side adapters?)

recommendations.rationale: Current table lists 8 adapters; readers don't know which are actively used vs deprecated (goose.nu, opencode.nu marked unclear)
